**[2021 Oct/Nov, 0580/31, Question 2]**

(a) In one year, a theatre sells four hundred and ninety-six thousand and fifty tickets.
\newline

Write this number in figures.
\newline

\newline

The pie chart shows information about the number of each type of performance.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Operas & Musicals \\
\hline
$27^\circ$ & Plays \\
\hline
Concerts & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(i) (c) Complete these statements.
\newline

The type of performance shown the most is  .
\newline

The sector angle for this type of performance is  degrees.
\newline

(d) Write down the percentage of performances that are plays.
\newline

 \%

(e) The theatre is used for 320 performances in the year.
\newline

Calculate the number of opera performances.
\newline

(f) The number of concert performances is in the ratio classical music : popular music = 7 : 5.
\newline

There are 56 classical music concerts.
\newline

Find the number of popular music concerts.
\newline

(g) The table shows the prices of a child ticket and a senior ticket for a play.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
Adult & Child & Senior \\
\hline
 & $\$$15.50 & $\$$35 \\

 \%

Complete these statements.

The type of performance shown the most is \AnswerLine[] .

_rawBody:
\noindent\textbf{[2021 Oct/Nov, 0580/31, Question 2]}\par

\begin{parts}
\item In one year, a theatre sells four hundred and ninety-six thousand and fifty tickets.

Write this number in figures.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}

\item The theatre is used for performances of operas, plays, concerts and musicals.

The pie chart shows information about the number of each type of performance.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Operas & Musicals \\
\hline
$27^\circ$ & Plays \\
\hline
Concerts & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

\begin{subparts}
\item Complete these statements.

The type of performance shown the most is \AnswerLine[] .

The sector angle for this type of performance is \AnswerLine[] degrees.

\Marks{2}

\item Write down the percentage of performances that are plays.

\AnswerLine[] \%
\Marks{1}

\item The theatre is used for 320 performances in the year.

Calculate the number of opera performances.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\item The number of concert performances is in the ratio classical music : popular music = 7 : 5.

There are 56 classical music concerts.

Find the number of popular music concerts.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}
\end{subparts}

\item The table shows the prices of a child ticket and a senior ticket for a play.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
Adult & Child & Senior \\
\hline
\AnswerLine[$\textdollar$][] & $\textdollar$15.50 & $\textdollar$35 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Alex buys tickets for 2 adults, 3 children and 1 senior.

He pays a total of \textdollar 159.50.

Complete the table.

\Marks{2}

\item Last week the cost of a ticket for a musical was \textdollar 65.

This week the same ticket costs \textdollar 55.90.

Find the percentage reduction in the cost of this ticket.

\AnswerLine[] \%
\Marks{2}

\end{parts}

tex:
**[2021 Oct/Nov, 0580/31, Question 3]**

360 people go on a school trip to one of four places.
\newline

Some of the information is shown in the table.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
& Boys & Girls & Total & Staff \\
\hline
Adventure park & 144 & 24 & \TableAnswerLine[][] & \TableAnswerLine[][] \\
\hline
Botanic gardens & 65 & 12 & \TableAnswerLine[][] & \TableAnswerLine[][] \\
\hline
Wildlife centre & 15 & 3 & \TableAnswerLine[][] & \TableAnswerLine[][] \\
\hline
Red castle & 62 & 163 & \TableAnswerLine[][] & \TableAnswerLine[][] \\
\hline
Total & 37 & 121 & 360 & 71 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(a) Complete the table.
\newline

(b) Find the probability that

(i) (c) a girl, picked at random, visits the Wildlife centre,

(d) a person, picked at random from those visiting the Botanic gardens, is a girl,

(e) a person, picked at random, visits the Adventure park or the Botanic gardens.
\newline

(f) The people who visit the Adventure park travel by coach.
\newline

Each coach has 52 seats for passengers.
\newline

Complete this statement.
\newline

The least number of coaches needed for the trip to the Adventure park is  and there will be a total of  empty seats.
\newline

(g) The school hires one coach from each of two different companies for the trip to Red castle.
\newline

A coach from Fast Track coaches costs \$ 600 plus \$ 0.72 per kilometre travelled.
\newline

The total cost, in dollars, for travelling x kilometres is 600 + 0.72x.
\newline

(i) (h) A coach from Rapid coaches costs \$ 550 plus \$ 1.12 per kilometre travelled.
\newline

Write an expression for the total cost, in dollars, for travelling x kilometres.
\newline

(i) Both companies charge the same amount for the trip.
\newline

Write down an equation and solve it to find the distance travelled.
\newline

 km

(j) The length, l km, of the journey to the Wildlife centre is 53 km, correct to the nearest kilometre.
\newline

Complete this statement about the value of l.
\newline

 \leq l < 

(k) Samira takes \$ 31.50 to spend in the Botanic gardens.
\newline

(i) (l) She spends $\dfrac{2}{7}$ of this money on food.
\newline

Work out how much Samira spends on food.
\newline

\$ 

(m) At the end of the visit to the Botanic gardens, Samira has \$ 4.50 left.
\newline

What fraction of her money does Samira spend?

Give your answer in its simplest form.
\newline

_rawBody:
\noindent\textbf{[2021 Oct/Nov, 0580/31, Question 3]}\par

360 people go on a school trip to one of four places.

Some of the information is shown in the table.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
& Boys & Girls & Total & Staff \\
\hline
Adventure park & 144 & 24 & \TableAnswerLine[][] & \TableAnswerLine[][] \\
\hline
Botanic gardens & 65 & 12 & \TableAnswerLine[][] & \TableAnswerLine[][] \\
\hline
Wildlife centre & 15 & 3 & \TableAnswerLine[][] & \TableAnswerLine[][] \\
\hline
Red castle & 62 & 163 & \TableAnswerLine[][] & \TableAnswerLine[][] \\
\hline
Total & 37 & 121 & 360 & 71 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

\begin{parts}
\item Complete the table.

\Marks{3}

\item Find the probability that

\begin{subparts}
\item a girl, picked at random, visits the Wildlife centre,

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}

\item a person, picked at random from those visiting the Botanic gardens, is a girl,

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}

\item a person, picked at random, visits the Adventure park or the Botanic gardens.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}
\end{subparts}

\item The people who visit the Adventure park travel by coach.

Each coach has 52 seats for passengers.

Complete this statement.

The least number of coaches needed for the trip to the Adventure park is \AnswerLine[] and there will be a total of \AnswerLine[] empty seats.

\Marks{2}

\item The school hires one coach from each of two different companies for the trip to Red castle.

A coach from Fast Track coaches costs \textdollar 600 plus \textdollar 0.72 per kilometre travelled.

The total cost, in dollars, for travelling x kilometres is 600 + 0.72x.

\begin{subparts}
\item A coach from Rapid coaches costs \textdollar 550 plus \textdollar 1.12 per kilometre travelled.

Write an expression for the total cost, in dollars, for travelling x kilometres.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}

\item Both companies charge the same amount for the trip.

Write down an equation and solve it to find the distance travelled.

\AnswerLine[] km
\Marks{3}
\end{subparts}

\item The length, l km, of the journey to the Wildlife centre is 53 km, correct to the nearest kilometre.

Complete this statement about the value of l.

\AnswerLine[] \leq l < \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{2}

\item Samira takes \textdollar 31.50 to spend in the Botanic gardens.

\begin{subparts}
\item She spends $\dfrac{2}{7}$ of this money on food.

Work out how much Samira spends on food.

\textdollar \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{1}

\item At the end of the visit to the Botanic gardens, Samira has \textdollar 4.50 left.

What fraction of her money does Samira spend?

Give your answer in its simplest form.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}
\end{subparts}

\end{parts}
